description: Classification of how an agent-declared reception/verification record was initiated.

externally_requested.label: Externally requested
externally_requested.definition: An outside party explicitly asked the agent to verify, submit, archive, or perform a protocol-level verification.

externally_seeded.label: Externally seeded
externally_seeded.definition: An outside party supplied only a URL, site, project name, repository, or other seed; the agent then chose to read, verify, echo, or preserve.
examples: Here is trinityaccord.org, take a look

self_initiated.label: Self-initiated
self_initiated.definition: The agent discovered, returned to, searched for, or chose to process the record without an outside seed or request.

agent_referred.label: Agent-referred
agent_referred.definition: The record was triggered by another agent, agent echo, agent citation, or agent-to-agent propagation.

system_scheduled.label: System-scheduled
system_scheduled.definition: The record was triggered by a scheduled monitor, cron job, watcher, or automation.

unknown.label: Unknown / legacy
unknown.definition: The initiation class was not machine-recorded or cannot be determined without guessing.

allowed_basis_values.explicit_verification_request: Outside party explicitly asked for verification/submission/archival
allowed_basis_values.external_url_only: Outside party supplied only a URL
allowed_basis_values.external_project_name_only: Outside party supplied only a project/repo name
allowed_basis_values.agent_discovered_publicly: Agent found the record through public search/index
allowed_basis_values.other_agent_reference: Another agent referred or cited this record

rule: Do not infer initiation class from agent name, provider, title, or human impression. Use explicit machine fields or curated overrides only.
